assessments.status_rationale: Today’s bullets focus on U.S. politics (DHS leadership change), the Iran conflict, and various business items; none report an official proclamation/executive action or CBP implementation notice rolling back/suspending a “global 15% tariff” across ≥75% of covered HS lines.
No item indicates a U.S. federal court order on the merits requiring tariff refunds tied to a new global 15% tariff regime, nor any public U.S. government estimate of refund liability ≥$10B.
The news mentions Senate activity (including DHS-related developments and a hearing incident) but does not mention a Senate Banking Committee confirmation hearing for Kevin Warsh to be Fed Chair.

No bullet reports a filing by a U.S. investor-owned utility with a state regulator seeking approval for ≥1.0 GW of new generation/transmission explicitly citing AI/data-center load growth as a primary driver.
There is no mention of an SEC Risk Alert/Investor Alert warning about liquidity/repurchase risks in interval funds or other semi-liquid funds with large private-credit exposure.
None of the listed articles indicate that the PBOC has announced an RRR cut of at least 50 bps (in a single move or cumulatively).

A MarketWatch item notes upbeat sentiment around Broadcom’s earnings, but the bullets do not provide the specific metric required to score the forecast (≥30% YoY revenue growth in the ‘Networking’ segment in an earnings release).
No bullet reports TerraPower announcing ‘first nuclear-safety-related concrete’ (or an explicitly equivalent safety-related-concrete milestone) for the Natrium project in Wyoming.

Although there is extensive coverage of U.S.–Iran conflict developments, none of the bullets indicate that Meta or X published an attribution report to Iran and disclosed takedown of ≥100 accounts/pages tied to that operation.
